pattern: A board square is white exactly when its column index and row index have different parities, computable directly from the two coordinate characters.
hints: Convert the file letter and the rank digit into numbers.
Squares alternate color, so the color depends only on whether the sum of the two indices is even or odd.
Return true when (column - 'a') and (row - '1') have opposite parity, i.e. their sum is odd.

stress.description: every valid chessboard coordinate exactly once (deterministic full sweep)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    files = 'abcdefgh'
    ranks = '12345678'
    f = files[rng.randrange(8)]
    r = ranks[rng.randrange(8)]
    return [f + r]
